posts.text:
Nadella: “potential” [presents no decisive results whatsoever]

Influencer: “It’s over man. It’s really over”

gets 300k views

🙄🤦‍♂️🙄 

What has happened to this place?


Ajey (@Paimaamu)

It’s over man. It’s really over.



— https://nitter.net/Paimaamu/status/2033194404144386172#m

The abstract of the interesting paper below is very reasonable.

The summary is totally over the top alarmist and anthropomorphic.

I am getting tired of tweets like these.

Pity the algorithm loves them.


Nav Toor (@heynavtoor)

🚨SHOCKING: 40 researchers from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, and Meta published a joint warning.

The AI you talk to every day is hiding what it is actually thinking.

And the window to do anything about it may be closing.

Here is what they found.

You know that

🔥


Prof. Lee Cronin (@leecronin)

Using chatgpt & alpha fold to make a cancer vaccine makes no sense on so many levels but of course everyone will believe it.



— https://nitter.net/leecronin/status/2033206068822569039#m

We could have just written a fat check to Russia and saved a lot of death and destruction.


van00sa (@van00sa)

Russian energy revenues were down nearly 50% year on year. Now Moscow is making an extra $150 million a day. Estimates are that becomes $3.3 to $5 billion in additional revenue by the end of this month.



— https://nitter.net/van00sa/status/2032391277421920261#m

Nope, @elonmusk, not even close. Software has not been eaten by AI.

Nobody has replaced an operating systems, or Excel or GPS or their favorite browser or favorite AAA video game entirely with AI. The results just aren’t high enough calibre.

Sure, you can use AI for prototyping. And you can use it for simple “CRUD” [Create, Replace, Update and Delete] databases, databases, knockoffs of existing codebases, etc.

But most complex* real world cases, custom software that has been developed over th

Dear @sama, 

You owe me an apology. You have relentlessly, publicly and privately, attacked my integrity and wisdom since my 2022 paper “Deep Learning is a Hitting a Wall”.  

But in your own way you have just come around to conceding *exactly* what I was arguing in that paper: that current architectures are not enough, and that we need something new, researchwise. beyond a scaling (a “megabreakthough” in your words below).

That’s all I was trying to say.  

And I was right. 

And you should b

Misleading summary. Should be deleted.

Altman doesn’t say a (known) new architecture is coming; he says he anticipates one will come someday.

PS: I also think we need something radical and new. In fact that’s what I’ve been saying for the last decade. 

Excess focus on exploiting LLMs has likely delayed discovery.


Rohan Paul (@rohanpaul_ai)

Sam Altman just said in his new interview, that a new AI architecture is coming that will be a massive upgrade, just like Transformers were over Long Sh

FACT: The hypothesis that “Scaling is all you need” for AGI has been the costliest mistake in scientific history.


Gary Marcus (@GaryMarcus)

Scaling is not all you need, illustration #3761: two of the two richest guys in the world tried to build AGI with basically unlimited budgets … and failed.



— https://nitter.net/GaryMarcus/status/2032877087850377721#m

Scaling is not all you need, illustration #3761: two of the two richest guys in the world tried to build AGI with basically unlimited budgets … and failed.


Amy Siskind 🏳️‍🌈 (@Amy_Siskind)

Worth mentioning that two of the AI players basically blew themselves up this week:
* Musk said he would basically start brand new on XAI after most of his cofounders left and his product is basically crap.
* Meta also shared that it’s AI is not working properly, after Zuckerberg spent hundreds of billions h
